newClaims.quote: Commit to a minimum of 100 service hours during Fall/Spring session which runs September 1-April 30, or 60 service hours during Summer session which runs May 1-August 31

newClaims.limitations: Volunteer (non-medical) commitment, not a USCE rotation duration.
newClaims.whyA1Missed: A1 focused on age eligibility and non-medical scope; service-hour commitment language was skipped.
newClaims.tierAssignmentRationale: Belongs to non-medical hospital volunteer commitment; not Tier 1 USCE rotation length.

newClaims.quote: If selected, commitment to complete a TGH Team Member Health Clearance, background check, and online orientation within designated time period following selection emailProfessional

newClaims.quote: Applications due by noon on March 1, July 1Applications reviewed on March 1-15, July 1-15Volunteers begin by May 1, September 1

a1ClaimsToRefine.explanation: Quote 'non-medical comfort and care' describes volunteer scope but is not an explicit refusal of observers/medical students. It should not be promoted to a NEGATIVE_NO_OBSERVERSHIP refusal — it is absence/scope-mismatch, not an explicit 'we do not accept observers' sentence.

additionalUnresolveds: Page does not state whether medical, premed, or IMG applicants may apply to this volunteer program in any clinical-observation capacity — silence, not refusal.
recommendedA3Focus: Confirm no PUBLIC_SAFE_USCE claim is emitted from this VOLUNTEER_SHADOW page; all claims must remain HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED or NO_PUBLIC_OPPORTUNITY_FOUND.
Verify TGH observership/visiting-student pathway is captured from a separate Education/USF Morsani-affiliation source, not this volunteer URL.

newClaims.quote: Our nurse residency program is designed to help recent nursing graduates smoothly transition into their first role as a registered nurse (RN). We also welcome and support nurses who have less than one year of hospital experience. Interested candidates can explore our nurse residency program jobs at www.tgh.org/careers and apply directly for the position and unit of their choice.
newClaims.claimText: TGH operates a nurse residency program for recent nursing graduates and RNs with <1 year hospital experience.
newClaims.tierAssignmentRationale: Nurse residency = post-graduate trainee program; Tier 2 trainee content.
newClaims.whyA1Missed: A1 emitted the navigation list of training programs but did not capture the substantive nurse residency program paragraph in the FAQ body.

newClaims.quote: Many of our team members support our patients without treating them in person. newClaims.tierAssignmentRationale: Remote-work characteristic of employment — Tier 3 career content.

newClaims.quote: Through a groundbreaking collaboration with USF Muma College of Business, we directly impact the professional growth of every one of our employees.

newClaims.quote: In general, TGH prioritizes offers of employment, and we aim to provide each applicant with an answer within approximately two weeks.

newClaims.whyA1Missed: A1 captured the 12-week contract RN paragraph but missed the separate float-pool/PRN opportunities paragraph.

a1ClaimsToRefine.explanation: Quote is a concatenated navigation header listing many subsections; it is not a substantive content statement and risks low-quality use downstream. Recommend regate scrutiny or replacement with more specific claims.
Quote concatenates 12 site-navigation program names without context; suitable as a Tier 2 inventory hint only. Each program should be sourced from its dedicated page during A3 follow-up.

additionalUnresolveds: Page references the Abdominal Transplant Fellowship and Pharmacy Residency Programs in nav but provides no details (PGY counts, ACGME status, eligibility) here.
Page mentions partial/full remote positions and corporate roles but does not enumerate physician-specific or visa-sponsorship policies; no J-1 waiver or H-1B language present.
Page asserts compliance with U.S. work authorization but does not state whether TGH sponsors employment visas for clinical/attending hires.
recommendedA3Focus: Confirm no PUBLIC_SAFE_USCE claim is emitted for this CAREERS_PORTAL scope source — all claims must be FUTURE_LANE_ONLY.
Verify the two distinct Talent Acquisition phone numbers (844-7551 and 844-7000) are both retained without dedup loss.
Confirm A1 claim tgh-careers-fl-001 and tgh-careers-006 are tagged as navigation-derived and not promoted to substantive program claims without follow-up source capture.

a1ClaimsToRefine.explanation: A1 reasonably flagged CAUTION_SAFE_INTERNAL_REVIEW. The link text is 'Physician-to-Physician Observer Application Request' which on its face restricts to credentialed physicians — IMG eligibility is not stated. Regate should not promote to PUBLIC_SAFE_USCE without the actual subpage.

f6 quote is 'USF Health Continuing Medical Education Program' — USF Health is the affiliated medical school, not TGH itself. Scope is more likely MEDICAL_SCHOOL_LEVEL or shared, not INSTITUTION_SPECIFIC.

additionalUnresolveds: Page is a Refer-a-Patient page with an Education nav listing; whether any of the listed fellowships, residencies, or observership pathways are open to IMGs is not stated on this source.
Physician Business Development lists named individuals (Christine Videtic, Erica Hammond, etc.) but no titles, contact emails, or roles are stated.
Page lists 'Careers' and 'Join Our Team' but provides no physician/APP job categories, J-1/H-1B sponsorship, or compensation information.
recommendedA3Focus: Verify no PUBLIC_SAFE_USCE claim is emitted from this Refer-a-Patient page — content is navigation-only with no observership eligibility or application detail.
Confirm f6 (USF Health CME) is not labeled INSTITUTION_SPECIFIC to TGH without scope review; this is jointly-branded with the USF medical school.
Confirm c1 observership claim remains CAUTION_SAFE_INTERNAL_REVIEW and is not promoted — 'Physician-to-Physician' wording suggests credentialed-physician-only audience.
